caption: Heirloom tomatoes coming in heavy today. Half going to our chef list, half to the weekend market. Tomato sandwich season.
Pour-over and quiet before the line comes in. Best hour of the day.
Duck breast, apple gastrique, charred scallion. Tonight's special. Don't sleep on stone fruit with fat.
Fresh wheel of aged tomme just cut. Grassy, nutty, ready for boards this weekend. Farmstead cheese at its best.
Stock day. Roasted bones, mirepoix, patience. The base of every plate starts here. No shortcuts.
Day-boat fluke, market this morning. This is what dawn pickups look like.
Pasta extrusion practice on a day off. Bronze die rigatoni holding sauce like a dream.
Market run before dawn. Butchers pulling day-boat catch. This is why we don't sleep in.
Sunday sauce, family recipe, family kitchen. Some things you don't improve, you just don't ruin.
Local corn, brown butter, lime. Simple but it has to be perfect. On the specials this week.
Cultured butter, hand-worked. Ready for restaurant partners this week.
Staff family meal: braised short rib over polenta. Fed people feed people better.
Pick-your-own apples opening tomorrow — bring the kids, bring bags.
Walking rows with a Hudson Valley grower. Know your food, know your farmer.
Live-fire brisket, oak coals, patience. Sometimes the meat tells you when it's done.
New wheel of raw-milk brie coming out of the aging room next week. Booking wholesale orders now.
